title: Cisco ISE Profiler Probe Quality and Endpoint-Classification Coverage
dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Profiler* category — codes `80001` (endpoint profiled), `80002` (endpoint reprofiled), `80003` (probe failure), `80004` (probe rate-limit), `80010` (profiler-feed update). Probes covered: DHCP, RADIUS, SNMP, NMAP, NetFlow, HTTP, DNS, AD, Active Probing. Field: `probe_type`, `endpoint_mac`, `device_type`, `confidence_score`. ISE OpenAPI `/api/v1/profile/feed` polled every 6h, written to `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:profiler_feed`.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (80001, 80003, 80004))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:profiler_feed")
| stats count, dc(endpoint_mac) as unique_endpoints, count(eval(MessageCode=="80003")) as probe_failures, count(eval(MessageCode=="80004")) as rate_limits, avg(confidence_score) as avg_confidence by probe_type
| eval health = case(probe_failures > 0 OR rate_limits > 0, "degraded", avg_confidence < 70, "low_confidence", true(), "healthy")
| where health != "healthy"
description: Detects probe failures, rate-limits, and low-confidence endpoint classifications across ISE's profiler probe types. When probes are unhealthy, endpoints are mis-classified, leading to wrong authorization profiles.
value: Profiler is how ISE knows what kind of device just authenticated — printer, IP phone, IoT sensor, laptop. Wrong classification means wrong authorization profile (and possibly wrong VLAN, wrong dACL). Catching probe degradation preserves classification fidelity.
implementation: Confirm *Profiler* category at INFO. Schedule every 30 min, search window `-2h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0.
visualization: (1) Per-probe health tile. (2) Confidence score distribution per probe. (3) Top-N: device types with low confidence.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **NMAP probe firewall block** — Some networks block active scanning; tag and exclude. (b) **DHCP probe coverage gap** — DHCP probe requires SPAN or DHCP relay; coverage may be partial. (c) **NetFlow probe rate-limit during traffic peak** — During a traffic spike, NetFlow probe may be rate-limited; expected.
mitreAttack: N/A (data-quality monitoring — measures ISE profiler probe reliability, no adversary technique)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(d) — access control. Profiler classification feeds access-control policy.
AC-3 (Access Enforcement) — profile-driven enforcement requires profiler health.
A.8.18 (Privileged utilities) — profiler is privileged-info source.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Disable the DHCP probe on a PSN. Within 30 min, classification confidence drops. UC-17.1.55 fires showing `dhcp` probe with `health=degraded`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Re-enable the probe. Health returns to 'healthy'.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE Profiler enabled with multiple probe types active.
• `device_inventory.csv` for cross-reference.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Profiler* category at INFO.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 30 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Profiler Probe Health":
• Per-probe health tile.
• Confidence distribution.
• Top-N low-confidence device types.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **All probes show degraded** — PSN resource saturation; cross-ref UC-17.1.29.
• **DHCP probe absent** — DHCP relay not configured to forward DHCP to PSN.
• **NMAP probe disabled** — Either by policy (firewall) or PSN config.
grandmaExplanation: ISE figures out what each device is — phone, printer, laptop — using several different methods. We check each method is working; if one is broken, ISE may be guessing wrong about what kind of device just connected.
